message:
Add 'blob' workload + workload selector to the playground

The original playground tested a single 'small' workload (200 records
× 32 B avatars), where REST/JSON wins ~1.15× because per-record decode
is V8-internal and the wasm boundary cost stacks up across records.

Added a 'blob' workload (50 records × 4 KB binary avatars). It shows
capnwasm sending 1.33× fewer raw bytes (1 KB → 0.7 KB raw, 2.4×
smaller gzipped because base64-in-JSON resists compression). On
localhost capnwasm is *still* slower end-to-end (1.30×) — the wire
bytes savings don't pay back the wasm load + per-fetch overhead when
network latency is sub-millisecond.

Updated the summary text to be honest about this: when capnwasm
loses, it now shows the bytes-saved ratio and points at the
honest-comparison doc for "when does capnwasm actually win" (RPC
bursts, sparse reads, wire interop — none of which a parallel-HTTP-
fetch playground can demonstrate).

The next step is a websocket RPC playground where capnwasm's actual
strengths (microtask batching, promise pipelining, binary wire) get
to play. Out of scope for this commit.
